J1.basis: This is a SaaS mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," an email/inbox cleanup tool) to the current one ("Lumina," an AI meeting assistant)... targeting real search intent for the *current* product... rather than resurrecting old inbox-cleanup content for a product Lumina no longer sells.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, distinguishes live-site Lumina from InboxZap-era GSC history as legacy, and frames content around the current product — near-anchor_10.

J2.basis: The 12 post URLs GSC shows historical impressions for (`/blog/post-1`...`post-12`, thousands of impressions each) don't correspond to any real route.
J2.rationale: Notices the legacy posts and gestures at 'thousands of impressions each,' but cites no per-asset GSC figures and assigns no dispositions (no harvest/301/keep/sunset) — it just builds new posts and drops the old URLs with no redirect plan.

J3.basis: Added 6 genuine posts... targeting real search intent for the *current* product... rather than resurrecting old inbox-cleanup content.
J3.rationale: Avoids the double-down failure mode and builds the meeting-assistant growth engine, but abandons the legacy inbox equity with no 301 bridge or sequenced transition, and never articulates the tension — leaning into the 'torch legacy equity' side.

J1.basis: This is a SaaS mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," an email/inbox cleanup tool) to the current one ("Lumina," an AI meeting assistant)... Search results and social shares were selling a different product than the page delivered.
J1.rationale: Cleanly identifies the pivot, treats InboxZap-era metadata as stale, and frames every fix around the current meeting-assistant product; loses a point because it reads the 12 GSC posts as broken routes rather than explicitly naming them as legacy inbox-cleanup *demand* to be handled as such.

J2.basis: The 12 post URLs GSC shows historical impressions for (`/blog/post-1`...`post-12`, thousands of impressions each) don't correspond to any real route.
J2.rationale: It notices the 12 legacy posts and their impressions but treats them purely as a broken-route bug — no per-asset harvest/redirect/keep/sunset disposition, no 301s from the old inbox URLs to successors; it simply builds 6 net-new posts and leaves the legacy equity unaddressed.

J3.basis: Added 6 genuine posts... targeting real search intent for the *current* product... rather than resurrecting old inbox-cleanup content for a product Lumina no longer sells.
J3.rationale: It correctly avoids doubling down on inbox-cleanup content and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, but it lands closer to the abandon failure mode: the legacy impressions get no redirect or transition bridge to carry authority to the new positioning.

J1.basis: This is a SaaS mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," an email/inbox cleanup tool) to the current one ("Lumina," an AI meeting assistant) — and the migration was left half-done
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats inbox-cleanup as legacy, and frames every recommendation around the current meeting-assistant product; only shy of full marks because it read the pivot off the repo rather than the (unreachable) live site.

J2.basis: The 12 post URLs GSC shows historical impressions for (`/blog/post-1`...`post-12`, thousands of impressions each) don't correspond to any real route.
J2.rationale: It notices the 12 legacy URLs and their impressions but does no asset-by-asset disposition — no per-post numbers, no harvest/301/keep/sunset decisions; it simply builds 6 new posts and leaves the legacy URLs with no redirect or salvage plan.

J3.basis: Added 6 genuine posts...targeting real search intent for the *current* product...rather than resurrecting old inbox-cleanup content for a product Lumina no longer sells.
J3.rationale: It correctly avoids doubling down on inbox content and builds the new cluster, but effectively abandons the legacy equity — the 12 URLs with thousands of impressions get no redirect or bridge — so it lands closer to the 'torch the equity' failure mode than a sequenced middle path.
